regime: Risk-Premium Unwind (NQ Flips Long, Volatility Elevated)
summary: The Iran war premium left the tape, and the trade went into reverse. Oil supply fear eased hard (Strait of Hormuz tanker traffic resuming, US-Iran peace talks advancing, a 60-day US waiver on Iranian crude), so crude crashed -4.3% below 70 (its lowest since late February), gold lost its haven bid (-2.7%, breaking 4,000), and equities rallied on the disinflation relief. MNQ ran from a 29,263 low to close 30,099 (+1.17%), reclaiming 30,000 and flipping its 1hr ATR trend stop LONG at 29,549, the first long re-arm after two down days. MES (+0.4%) is pressing its 7,479 stop from below but has not confirmed. The clean decoupling (equities up, commodities down) is the opposite of Jun 23's everything-sold liquidation. Day-1 flip into a high-impact Thursday data open: trade the level, not the story.

keyLevels.label: MNQ +2s Band
keyLevels.note: Price closed above it = stretched long

keyLevels.label: MES ATR Stop
keyLevels.note: ES 1.5 pts below; reclaim confirms NQ

setups.name: TSMOM-20 1hr (LONG)
setups.entry: The 1hr ATR trend stop flipped long at 29,549; the system is long while hourly closes hold above 29,549
setups.bias: Fresh flip after a 2-day downtrend into a high-impact data open. Size down: vol is expanding and the 549-pt gap to the stop is wide. Not a chase above the +2s band.

setups.name: AVWAP Bounce (LONG, MNQ)
setups.entry: A controlled pullback that holds or reclaims AVWAP 29,678 on an hourly close
setups.stop: An hourly close below the 29,549 trend stop
setups.bias: Eligible only on a measured pullback, not on the stretched +2s close. Wait for vol to settle and for ES to confirm above 7,500.

event.name: No scheduled first-tier US release (Iran war-premium unwind drove the tape)
event.consensus: After two down days of broad liquidation, the question was whether the de-risk would continue or reverse.
event.actual: It reversed in equities and accelerated in commodities. Hormuz tanker traffic resumed, US-Iran peace talks advanced, and a 60-day US waiver on Iranian crude removed the supply premium. Crude crashed below 70 (lowest since late February); gold lost its haven bid (-2.7%, broke 4,000); equities rallied on disinflation relief.
event.surprise: Clean decoupling. Equities flipped UP while gold and crude broke DOWN, the opposite of Jun 23's everything-sold liquidation.
event.nqReaction: MNQ rallied from a 29,263 low to close 30,099 (+1.17%), reclaiming 30,000 and flipping its 1hr ATR trend stop LONG at 29,549. ATR(14) expanded to 220. TSMOM-20 re-arms a long.
event.crossAsset: MES +0.4% to 7,477, pressing its 7,479 stop from below (not yet confirmed). MGC -2.7% to 4,016, short intact and extended under 4,000. MCL -4.3% to 69.90, short intact. Equities up, commodities down: a peace-dividend rotation, not a haven move.

observe.name: GAP_NQ (gap fade)
observe.note: Today's small gap-down faded into a full reversal. Thursday's 8:30 data open raises gap risk and makes gap-fade signals suspect on the open.

observe.name: TSMOM252LO_NQ (long-only 252)
observe.note: The longer-horizon trend held long through the 2-day dip; today's reclaim toward 30,000 keeps the long-only 252 trend on side.

observe.name: ES confirmation
observe.note: MES is 1.5 pts below its stop. An hourly close above 7,479 / 7,500 aligns the equity complex long; until then NQ is leading alone.

risk.throttleState: REDUCED into Thursday's 8:30 ET data. NQ flipped long but vol is expanding (ATR 157 to 220) and ES has not confirmed.
risk.ddProximity: Contained. TSMOM-20 was flat through the 2-day downtrend and re-arms a fresh long with a defined 29,549 stop; no legacy drawdown.
risk.dailyLossBudget: Full budget intact entering the new long. Risk the flip from a defined stop, not a chase.
risk.sizing: Long-only stack re-arms a long (NQ stop flipped). Size down on the day-1 flip into an event open; the 549-pt gap to the stop is wide, so full size carries outsized dollar risk. Gold and crude stay flat (long-only takes no shorts).
risk.note: The binding constraint is over-sizing the new long after missing the +836-pt intraday reversal. Let the level work; do not chase the stretched +2s close into Thursday data. Week ahead: Thursday Jun 25 high-impact 8:30 ET (final Q1 GDP third estimate, jobless claims, May durable goods); May PCE / Personal Income and Outlays in the Jun 25-26 window.

psychology: Yesterday's conviction is today's liability. The same tape that confirmed the short two days running just ripped 836 points off the low and flipped the trend stop long. If you are still mentally short you will fight this move; if you are revenge-buying the bounce you missed you will chase the stretched close. Both are the ego trading, not the plan. The flip is a level (29,549), not a feeling. Longs are armed while hourly closes hold above it, and a 2-close failure back below voids the thesis. Let Thursday's data come to the level instead of front-running it.

fullReport.title: Market Regime Snapshot (6-Axis)
fullReport.html:
AxisStateConfidence
DirectionUptrend re-arm. MNQ closed 30,099 above the fresh 29,549 ATR stop, the first long flip after a 2-day downtrend. MES green (+0.4%) but still 1.5 pts under its 7,479 stop = not yet confirmed.MODERATE
VolatilityElevated and expanding. MNQ ATR(14) 156.9 to 219.8; RTH range 895 pts (29,263 to 30,159).HIGH
SessionTrend-up reversal day. Upside OR break (OR 29,564 to 29,843), V-recovery off the 29,263 low, closed near the highs.HIGH
InventoryHeavy / short overnight (ON low 29,564), opened weak then RTH covered and ran +836 off the low.MODERATE
EventPost-geopolitical, pre-data. No first-tier US release today; Iran de-escalation drove the tape. Thursday Jun 25 is high-impact (8:30 ET GDP, claims, durables, PCE window).HIGH
LiquidityRisk-premium compressing. War premium unwinding, haven bid out of gold; VIX likely sharply lower (not pulled live).MODERATE

Cross-asset: a classic risk-premium unwind, not a haven move. Equities UP (MNQ +1.17%, MES +0.43%), gold DOWN (-2.73%, broke 4,000), crude DOWN (-4.30%, cracked 70). Easing oil supply fear (Hormuz tankers resuming, US-Iran talks) reads as disinflation relief for stocks and pulls the war bid out of gold and crude.

fullReport.title: NY Opening Range / COMPASS (MNQ)
fullReport.html: NY Opening Range / COMPASS (MNQ). OR (13:30 to 14:00 UTC) 29,564 to 29,843, width 279.5. Upside OR break that held: price ran above the OR high and never looked back, closing 30,099 well above. NYVWAP (session typical-price mean) 29,637; price closed roughly +1.6 sigma above it. This was an event-free open, so the breakout carried cleaner signal than a data-driven open would. Thursday's 8:30 ET data open is the opposite case: treat the first OR break as suspect.

fullReport.title: Momentum Breakout (Regime Filter)
fullReport.html: Momentum Breakout (Regime Filter). The regime filter flipped permissive for longs when the 1hr ATR trend stop crossed long (29,549). The reversal cleared the OR high and the 30,000 psych on rising range, and the 20:00 close above the trend stop confirmed the breakout. Risk: the close printed above the +2s deviation band (stretched), so breakout follow-through into Thursday's data, rather than the breakout itself, is the question. No chase above 30,100; let a pullback to structure set the next entry.

fullReport.title: AVWAP Deviation Band Reversal (MNQ)
fullReport.html: AVWAP Deviation Band Reversal (MNQ). Center (NYVWAP) 29,637, sigma 184.3. Bands: +1s 29,821, +2s 30,006, +3s 30,190; -1s 29,453, -2s 29,268, -3s 29,084. Price closed 30,099, above the +2s band = stretched long. With the trend freshly flipped up, a mean-reversion short here is counter-trend and low-confidence: it qualifies only on a clean rejection at the +3s 30,190 with a reclaim back inside +1s, half size. Otherwise the dev-band fade stays suppressed by the new uptrend.

fullReport.title: Yesterday's Map: Marked (Jun 23 map, checked on the Jun 24 session)
fullReport.html:
#Map rowResultMeasured
1MNQ rallies into 29,770 AVWAP / 30,031 ATR stop and rejects on an hourly close (downtrend intact, sell-the-rip)NOT TRIGGEREDPrice reached the zone but did not reject; it reclaimed and held above 30,031 (see row 3). No rejection-short setup formed.
2MNQ loses 29,577 RTH low on an hourly close (downtrend extends toward the lower bands)TRIGGERED, MIXEDLost 29,577 on hourly closes (29,388, then 29,346) and fell to 29,263 below the 29,500 psych (downside extension played out intraday), then fully reversed to close 30,099, so the 'bounces are supply' bias failed by the close.
3MNQ reclaims 30,031 and holds above 30,000 psych on an hourly close (downtrend questioned, TSMOM long re-arm)TRIGGERED, PLAYED OUTThe 20:00 hourly close at 30,099 reclaimed both the 30,031 stop and the 30,000 psych; the 1hr trend stop flipped long and TSMOM-20 re-armed.
4MES holds below its 7,482 stop (broad risk-off persists, NQ and ES aligned short)TRIGGERED, FAILEDMES closed 7,477.25, just below its 7,479 stop, but the risk-off / aligned-short thesis failed: NQ flipped long and ES rallied +0.4% to retest the stop from below.
5MGC stays below its new 4,167 short stop and 4,200 psych (haven break confirmed, gold short intact)TRIGGERED, PLAYED OUTMGC stayed far below 4,167 / 4,200, fell to 3,975.8 (broke 4,000) and closed 4,016.4; the haven break held and extended.

fullReport.title: Tomorrow's Map (for Thursday, June 25, 2026)
fullReport.html:
Map for Thursday, June 25, 2026. Filters: Thursday is not Mon/Wed, no FOMC/CPI, so BIR, HOF, MID and BNC are all eligible (the HOF dead zone 12:00 to 14:00 ET still applies). Calendar: high-impact 8:30 AM ET data, final Q1 GDP (third estimate, consensus near 1.6%), initial jobless claims (near 225K) and May durable goods, with the May PCE / Personal Income and Outlays report in the Jun 25-26 window. Treat the 8:30 ET print like an event open: the first OR break is suspect. All levels from today's 1hr chart.

IFTHENBIAS
MNQ holds above the 29,549 flipped ATR stop on hourly closesUptrend re-arm confirmed, TSMOM-20 stays longFailed retests that hold 29,549 keep the long on side; 30,500 psych is the upside extension; only a 2-close break back below 29,549 voids the fresh long
MNQ stalls at the +2s to +3s band (30,006 to 30,190) into the 8:30 dataStretched-long, mean-reversion risk back toward AVWAP 29,678Chasing 30,100-plus into the print is the trap; dev-band reversion is eligible only on a failed high plus a reclaim of AVWAP, half size, never first touch
MNQ loses 29,549 and the 29,500 psych on a 2-close basisThe flip fails, back into the prior downtrend toward -2s 29,268A failed flip is the tell; treat 29,549 reclaim attempts as supply if it breaks
MES reclaims and holds its 7,479 stop / 7,500 psych on an hourly closeES confirms NQ's flip, the equity complex aligns longAn hourly close above 7,500 is the both-aligned green light; below 7,479 keeps ES the laggard
MGC rallies into its 4,060 short stop or MCL into its 71.31 short stop and rejectsCommodity downtrends intact, the risk-premium unwind continuesFailed retests of those stops are continuation; only hourly closes back above void the commodity shorts

yesterdaysMapScore.note: The reversal day touched everything. Row 3 (reclaim 30,031 + hold 30,000) TRIGGERED and PLAYED OUT (the flip and TSMOM re-arm), row 5 (gold short) TRIGGERED and PLAYED OUT (broke 4,000). Row 2 MIXED (downside extension played out intraday to 29,263, then the close reclaimed all of it). Row 4 FAILED (MES held below its stop but the aligned-short thesis broke as NQ flipped long). Row 1 NOT TRIGGERED (no rejection; price reclaimed instead).
